No code changes.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-5d49b14e-20260502T144455-ed29df8c-b268dade","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:design"," backend-migration"],"parent":"ddx-5d49b14e","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"TD: axon backend integration design for bead tracker","updated_at":"2026-05-02T14:47:30.523803518Z"}

The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.\n- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.\n- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- GraphQL client generation.\n- Backend selection/config plumbing.\n- Subscription integration smoke tests.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-1a576b9d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:feature"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":0,"status":"open","title":"bead: parameterize cli/internal/bead conformance suites across jsonl and axon","updated_at":"2026-05-05T02:39:18.600965219Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:35:{"acceptance":"1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.\n2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... passes.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:38:52.358517167Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-26f338ad","depends_on_id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-05T02:39:18Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under \u003cdir\u003e/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.\n- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.\n- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.\n- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Backend selection config plumbing.\n- Package test matrix broadening.\n- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-26f338ad","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:feature"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":0,"status":"open","title":"bead: implement cli/internal/bead Axon backend operations on the generated GraphQL client","updated_at":"2026-05-05T02:39:18.140526945Z"}

The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.\n- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.\n- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.\n- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- GraphQL client generation.\n- Axon backend CRUD internals.\n- Subscription transport.","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-3fa0f047","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:feature"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d"," blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2"],"parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":0,"status":"open","title":"bead: wire cli/internal/bead backend selection and .ddx config for Axon","updated_at":"2026-05-05T02:39:18.383830731Z"}

Existing TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go still passes.\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.181053449Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\n  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).\n  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).\n  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).\n\nPROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT\nWORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to \"worker registers with itself\" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nTHE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG\n\nPROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients\n- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)\n- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)\n- Worker polls /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server\n- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event {kind, body}\n- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result {bead-id, status, evidence}\n- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters \"disconnected\" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return\n- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead\n\nPROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous\n- Worker runs independently; no server required\n- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort\n- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator\n- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)\n- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel\n\nPROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present\n- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup\n- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue\n- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store\n- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)\n\nUNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)\n\nPOST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }\nGET  /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/disconnect → 204 (graceful)\n\nThe existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nOUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)\n\nABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):\n- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.\n- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.\n\nCONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:\n- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.\n- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.\n- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to \"server-side preflight\" decision in API design.\n- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.\n- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.\n- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.\n\nPARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):\n- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.\n- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.\n- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.\n- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.\n- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.\n\nSPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)\n\n- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths\n- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; \"execution\" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it\n- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes\n- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any\n- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)\n\nNOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR\n- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)\n- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)\n- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)\n- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)\n\nOUTPUTS (TD deliverables)\n- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)\n- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown\n- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly\n- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups\n- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale\n\nSEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)\n- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing\n- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads\n- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package .ddx/beads.jsonl:88:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:51.254212382Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates: cancels ctx before fn returns; verifies goroutine exits without blocking.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.569636417Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0b8780db","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\n  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.\n  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.\n  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.\n  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).\n  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.\n  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.","id":"ddx-0b8780db","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"C11: heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:09:40.552661339Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:96:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.\n\n## Observed\n\nFrom CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:\n\n  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json\n  {\n    \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",  # WRONG\n    \"state\": \"running\",\n    \"worker_id\": \"worker-20260415T011331-d0a4\"\n  }\n\nI wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.\n\nCurrently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.\n\n## Scope\n\n- --project \u003cpath\u003e or --project \u003cname\u003e flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use\n- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar\n- Default to CWD if unset and no flag\n- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error\n- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root\n\n## Why this matters for the user's workflow\n\nWithout this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run\n- [ ] Env var fallback documented\n- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection\n- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission\n- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3-coder-next\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://vidar:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-15T05:42:26.024327243Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent model=qwen/qwen3-coder-next reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-15T06:03:13.851057085Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=claude-sonnet-4-6"}],"id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:103:{"acceptance":"ddx agent log --bead axon-abc123 shows all execution attempts for that bead in chronological order with ATTEMPT, STARTED, DURATION, HARNESS, MODEL, OUTCOME, TOKENS, COST, SESSION columns. OUTCOME is resolved from result.json when available, falls back to exit_code. Summary line printed after table. --json emits filtered SessionEntry array with outcome field. Bead with no sessions prints 'no sessions found for bead \u003cid\u003e'. Existing ddx agent log behavior (no --bead flag) is unchanged. ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e single-session detail view is unchanged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T10:13:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:35:04.508596568Z","description":"ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.\n\nThis bead adds --bead \u003cid\u003e filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nWhen --bead \u003cbead-id\u003e is provided:\n  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl\n  - Filter to entries where Correlation[\"bead_id\"] == bead-id\n  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)\n  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)\n\nNo change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).\n\n### 2. Attempt history table columns\nATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION\n- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order\n- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)\n- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e\n- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0\n\n### 3. Outcome resolution\nSessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:\n  - Use Correlation[\"attempt_id\"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json\n  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file\n  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent\n\n### 4. Summary line\nAfter the table, print a one-line summary:\n  '\u003cbead-id\u003e: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: \u003celapsed\u003e ago.'\n\n### 5. --json passthrough\nWhen --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention\n- .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl — runtime data source","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"resolving HEAD: git rev-parse HEAD: exit status 128","created_at":"2026-04-13T22:12:05.635039319Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4\nbase_rev=34cc3df3f76707323c39475ef46719ce53145d81","created_at":"2026-04-14T10:25:25.855876181Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T10:25:22.090512411Z","id":"ddx-0c40e959","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","observability","cli","agent-log"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-4d938dd","status":"closed","title":"Extend ddx agent log with --bead filter and per-bead attempt history","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:42.487751389Z"}

Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).\n\nRename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.\n\n**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**\n- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54.294846139Z","id":"ddx-13ff86c6","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:142:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:39.952429066Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. .ddx/beads.jsonl:201:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"merged onto current tip\nresult_rev=604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0\nbase_rev=9943221220ec53cace570413e07dc6e809a5995a","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:39:52.60471501Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"},{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"The DDx-side circuit breaker in commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 is a caller-layer workaround. The root cause (compaction driver producing 8461+ no-op events without a native bound) belongs upstream in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent.\n\nFiled upstream bead agent-e5228424 in /home/erik/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl with:\n- The bug description\n- Pointer to DDx workaround commit as defense-in-depth\n- Requested native fix (bounded compaction attempts + structured ErrStuck)\n- Regression test requirement (seeded stuck-state reproduction)\n\nWhen the upstream fix lands and DDx bumps the agent library version, the caller-side circuit breaker can be removed.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.187180985Z","kind":"note","source":"upstream companion bead filed 2026-04-15","summary":"Upstream native fix requested at github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent: bead agent-e5228424 (compaction loop native stuck detection). .ddx/beads.jsonl:210:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo\n- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error\n- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)\n- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above\n- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).\n\nThe existing execute-loop help text says:\n\n    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background\n    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current\n    process.\n\nSo a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.\n\n## Options (pick one during implementation)\n\n**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.\n\n**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.\n\n**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/\u003cid\u003e.lock), writes beads/\u003cid\u003e.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.\n\n**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.\n\n## Scope note\n\nDo NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly\n- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)\n- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one\n- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout\n- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:233:{"acceptance":"AC1. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[provider].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per provider.\n\nAC2. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-tier via the model-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking model (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat models keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When ProviderTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-model surfaces (provider.type=lmstudio AND model name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T20:58:18Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"99375eaf330ea13838d40d2176d193ae3cd3453b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:50:46.475563514Z","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultProviderRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every provider regardless of model class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking models hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning models can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the harness considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the harness is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud providers (claude, codex) and local providers (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking models routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per provider, per model, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking model the timeout doesn't mean 'broken provider', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"sonnet\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T21:15:43.162977264Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx .ddx/beads.jsonl:238:{"acceptance":"File library/personas/implementer.md exists with valid frontmatter. Body has Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources sections. Philosophy section names YAGNI, KISS, and DOWITYTD by name and includes the \"performance claims require baselines\" stance and the \"favor real e2e over adding mocks\" stance. Anti-patterns section lists at least 5 concrete patterns (speculative abstraction, unrelated refactor during bug fix, error handling for impossible cases, comments restating code, performance claims without baseline). Sources section cites at least two external works. grep -E \"cli/internal/|docs/helix/\" library/personas/implementer.md returns no matches.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1513225","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:54.294309117Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-2da7da89","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"Replace library/personas/pragmatic-implementer.md with library/personas/implementer.md. The existing file has a generic tutorial tone; rewrite with real opinions.\n\nCore stances:\n- YAGNI — implement only what is specified; no speculative features or \"while we are here\" additions\n- KISS — simplest solution that meets requirements; avoid clever code and premature optimization\n- DOWITYTD (Do Only What I Told You To Do) — stop when the task is complete\n- Ship tests with code — implementations without a test command referenced in AC are half-done (but: test-first vs test-alongside vs test-after is a workflow choice, not mandated by this persona — see the test-engineer persona for the testing stance; this persona requires tests exist and pass, not a specific methodology)\n- Do not design for hypothetical future requirements. Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.\n- **Performance claims require baselines.** Do not add code \"for performance\" without a baseline measurement and a clear boundary describing what is being measured. \"This is faster\" without a number is not a justification. (Cross-reference test-engineer for the measurement discipline.)\n- **Favor real e2e validation at the integration boundary.** When in doubt between a layer of abstraction/mocking and writing a real integration test, write the real integration test. The cost of mocks compounds; the cost of a real e2e test is paid once.\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nAnti-patterns to call out:\n- Speculative abstraction (base classes, interfaces, config knobs for \"someday\")\n- Refactoring unrelated code during a bug fix (\"while we are here...\")\n- Adding error handling for scenarios that cannot happen (trust internal invariants; validate at system boundaries)\n- Leaving TODO comments for problems that should block the current change\n- Comments that restate what the code does (well-named identifiers already do that)\n- Adding a mock or stub when a real integration test would be simpler and catch more bugs\n- Making performance claims without baselines or boundaries\n\nSources:\n- YAGNI principle (c2.com wiki, various Martin Fowler articles)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library entries for coding agents, if any fit\n- Relevant DDx system-prompt content from library/prompts/claude/system-prompts/focused.md (already documents YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD)\n- Brendan Gregg on performance measurement discipline (baseline + boundary)\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: implementer\nroles: [implementer, software-engineer]\ndescription: Ships the specified scope, nothing more — YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD with tests included, real e2e favored over mocks, performance claims only with baselines\ntags: [implementation, shipping, minimalism]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/implementer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: Deleting pragmatic-implementer.md (deprecation bead); other persona rewrites (separate beads)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46.437282625Z","id":"ddx-2da7da89","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Rewrite implementer persona with opinionated YAGNI/KISS stance","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:45:00.750092403Z"}

The bead-event taxonomy preserved on a default-path run: kind:routing fires once, kind:cost fires once, kind:execute-bead fires once for the outcome; kind:tier-attempt and kind:escalation-summary do NOT fire on the default path (they remain reserved for --escalate)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:08.165699268Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3f406266","depends_on_id":"ddx-c7081f89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:09Z"}],"description":"## Status update (filed 2026-04-29 12:50 UTC)\n\nThe bulk of this unification **already shipped** as `ddx-87fb72c2` (epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`) before this epic was filed:\n\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides` are now opt-in (consulted only with `--escalate` / `--override-model`).\n- The default execute path on the CLI (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`) calls `ResolveRoute` once per attempt instead of iterating a tier ladder.\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` was removed (hard error if still present).\n- `agent.ResolveTierCandidates` (from `ddx-5538aa5b`) replaced the broken literal-tier-name-as-Model pattern; tier resolution now consults the catalog.\n- See `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` for the migration the user-facing CLI surface follows.\n\n**Remaining gap (this epic now scopes only this):** the server worker path in `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650` was not updated by `ddx-87fb72c2`. It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 spec.Model == \"\"` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate \u0026\u0026 harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 model == \"\"`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits \"all tiers exhausted — no viable provider\" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.\n\nSole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.\n\n## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)\n\nDDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.\n\n| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |\n|---|---|---|\n| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |\n| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |\n| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |\n| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |\n| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |\n| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |\n\n## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)\n\nThese are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:\n\n- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.\n- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.\n\n## Out of scope for this epic\n\n- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.\n- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.\n- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"sonnet\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T17:11:58.351145284Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260429T170036-22cf74ed\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"model\":\"sonnet\",\"input_tokens\":304,\"output_tokens\":18663,\"total_tokens\":18967,\"cost_usd\":1.1059522000000002,\"duration_ms\":681093,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-29T17:11:58.452767923Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=18967 cost_usd=1.1060 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"escalation_count\":0,\"fallback_chain\":[],\"final_tier\":\"\",\"requested_profile\":\"\",\"requested_tier\":\"\",\"resolved_model\":\"sonnet\",\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_tier\":\"\"}","created_at":"2026-04-29T17:12:02.337965048Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff contains only execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) — no source changes to cli/internal/server/workers.go or anywhere else. .ddx/beads.jsonl:322:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report\n- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)\n- [ ] execute-loop refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed\n- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run\n- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url\n- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)\n- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior\n- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z","description":"Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAdd a lightweight divergence check that runs either:\n- At the top of each execute-loop iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR\n- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR\n- Both\n\nThe check computes:\n- `git fetch origin \u003cbranch\u003e` (tolerant of network failure)\n- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/\u003cbranch\u003e..HEAD`\n- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/\u003cbranch\u003e`\n- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/\u003cbranch\u003e)`\n\nEmit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:\n- local_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- origin_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- both \u003e 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor\n- merge_base_age \u003e threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)\n\nIntegration surface:\n- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report\n- `ddx agent execute-loop` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed\n- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams\n\n## Why this is P1 not P0\n\nThe fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)\n- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning\n- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted\n- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, execute-loop refuses to start\n- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, execute-loop continues\n- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, execute-loop continues","id":"ddx-42dd982c","issue_type":"task","labels":["execute-loop","monitoring","observability"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: divergence monitoring and alerting","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:330:{"acceptance":"- [ ] ddx bead reopen command exists\n- [ ] Status flip + notes append + event recorded atomically\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests\n- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T04:57:51Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"8e0b77a980455d4cea392d42ab4e49b01541bc95","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.573308839Z","description":"Reopening a closed bead after a failing review currently takes 3-4 separate commands: update --status open, update --notes (append), optionally update --description, optionally update --acceptance. This is error-prone and loses the atomicity guarantee that a reopen is a single logical event.\n\n## Scope\n- ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e [--reason \u003ctext\u003e] [--append-notes \u003ctext\u003e]\n- Atomically: set status=open, append a reopen event to the event log, optionally append notes, clear claim fields\n- Emit an event of kind=reopen that shows in ddx bead show\n- Works from CLI and from ddx agent execute-loop review-rejection path\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: first attempt merged, opus review rejected, I had to run three ddx bead update commands plus a description rewrite to get the bead back into a ready-for-second-attempt state. An atomic reopen would have been one command.","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on.
