named offenders in DDx's test suite.\n- The word \"mock\" appears only in prohibitions and in the Drift Signals subsection. The body of the Unit/Integration/Contract sections uses \"stub,\" \"in-memory implementation,\" or \"real backend\" exclusively.\n\n(3) Source/installed sync:\n- library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md and .ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md are byte-identical after the fix.\n\n(4) Regression witness:\n- A new test fixture or smoke check somewhere in the repo (e.g., a lefthook pre-commit or a doc-validation script) greps library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md for the forbidden phrases above and fails the commit if any reappear. This prevents silent drift if someone later reverts.\n\n(5) Changelog entry or commit message:\n- The commit message explicitly references the HELIX testing concern and the DDx concerns.md override, explains why mocks are being removed from the persona, and cites at least one concrete DDx anti-pattern (orchTestGitOps / fakeExecuteBeadGit / TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace) that the old persona produced.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T15:25:37.692295461Z","description":"The test-engineer-tdd persona at library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md promotes mocks as standard practice, directly contradicting the HELIX testing concern that DDx declared active in concerns.md on 2026-04-05. Every agent that adopts this persona produces mock-heavy tests that violate the concern's rules. This is how DDx accumulated `orchTestGitOps`, `fakeExecuteBeadGit`, `gateTestGitOps`, `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace`, `mockExecutor`, and every other test-that-lies-about-its-coverage pattern the opus test plan review surfaced.\n\n## Provenance\n\n- Persona authored 2025-09-15 in commit a4cc8ac as part of FEAT-011 persona system build\n- Testing concern adopted in DDx 2026-04-05 in commit b711ac8 (`Add testing concern`)\n- The persona has been silently contradicting the concern for 9 days with no audit\n- Personas are a DDx-native concept (HELIX has no personas/ directory in workflows/); this is DDx's problem to fix\n\n## The core principle\n\n**Mocks are a worst practice and should be avoided in 99% of cases.** The right pattern, always, is: extract an interface at your domain boundary, write an in-memory stub implementation of that interface, and write a contract test suite that runs against BOTH the stub and the real backend. When both pass the same suite they are proven interchangeable. This exposes hidden assumptions, keeps stubs honest, and produces tests that assert behavior instead of call sequences.\n\nMocks — defined specifically as call-recording test doubles that assert method X was called Y times with args Z — test implementation instead of behavior. They break when internals change without user-visible consequences. They give false confidence: a passing mock test proves nothing about the system's actual behavior because the thing under test never interacts with a real dependency. Most notoriously in DDx: mock git tests have proven zero merge-race, rebase-conflict, or serialization invariants despite claiming to.\n\nThe 1% of cases where mocks are acceptable: third-party SDK boundaries where a stub is infeasible (payment processors with real-world-only callbacks, email delivery, SMS, external auth providers). Even there, prefer contract tests against a sandbox environment when one exists.\n\n## Offending sections\n\nlibrary/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md lines 90-94 (Unit Tests):\n\n```\n### Unit Tests\n- Test individual functions/methods in isolation\n- Mock all external dependencies\n- Focus on business logic\n- Use test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)\n```\n\n`Mock all external dependencies` is absolute and wrong. The HELIX testing concern says:\n\n\u003e Prefer stubs (canned return values) over mocks (call-sequence assertions). Stubs test behavior; mocks test implementation.\n\u003e Use real implementations where practical: real databases via testcontainers or Docker Compose, real HTTP via local servers, real file systems via temp directories.\n\u003e Mock only at true system boundaries: third-party APIs, payment processors, email services, external auth providers.\n\u003e Never mock the thing you are testing.\n\n`Use test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)` lists mocks alongside stubs as equivalent. They are not equivalent. Mocks assert call sequences (implementation detail). Stubs return canned values (behavior contract). The persona should never put them on the same line.\n\nlibrary/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md lines 96-100 (Integration Tests):\n\n```\n### Integration Tests\n- Test component interactions\n- Use real implementations where possible\n- Test database operations\n- Verify API contracts\n```\n\n`Use real implementations where possible` is weakly worded. DDx's project override in concerns.md states:\n\n\u003e Integration tests: zero mocks. No exceptions. Integration tests exercise real production implementations of every dependency in the graph — real git (t.TempDir() + scrubbed GIT_* env), real filesystem, real bead store, real coordinator, real server, real agent harness (the script harness is the only approved fake for the AI-provider boundary, and it does not mock — it performs real filesystem and git operations deterministically). A test that substitutes any mock for a production component is not an integration test. It is a unit test that is lying about its coverage.\n\nThe persona must echo this in absolute language, not \"where possible.\"\n\n## What to change\n\n### Rewrite the Unit Tests section\n\nReplace lines 90-94 with guidance that matches the HELIX concern and DDx override exactly. Required content:\n\n- The code under test depends on interfaces at domain boundaries. Extract the interface if it does not exist.\n- Isolate the code under test with in-memory STUB implementations of those interfaces. Stubs return canned values the test sets up. They never record call sequences.\n- Every stub must be backed by a contract test suite that the real implementation ALSO passes. Same suite, both implementations. That proves they are interchangeable.\n- Never use testify/mock, gomock, mockery-generated interfaces for assertion purposes, or any call-sequence assertion library. Assert on behavior — what did the code do to observable state? — not on call counts.\n- Never mock git, filesystem, bead store, database, queue, or any first-party infrastructure — use real implementations in temp directories.\n- The only approved \"fake\" in DDx unit tests is an in-memory implementation of a first-party interface you own.\n- Mocks are a worst practice. Avoid them in 99% of cases in favor of extracted interfaces with in-memory stubs. The 1% exception is third-party SDK boundaries where a stub is infeasible.\n\n### Rewrite the Integration Tests section\n\nReplace lines 96-100 with:\n\n- Integration tests use zero mocks. Period. No exceptions.\n- Real database in a temp dir, real filesystem via t.TempDir(), real git via git init in a temp dir with scrubbed GIT_* env, real HTTP via a local test server, real queues, real bead store, real coordinator, real server.\n- The only approved fake in the DDx suite is the `script` harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) as the AI-provider boundary replacement. It is not a mock — it performs real filesystem and git operations deterministically against the worktree.\n- A test that substitutes any mock for a production component is not an integration test. It is a unit test that is lying about its coverage.\n- Never mock the thing you are testing. A coordinator test that substitutes a fake LandingGitOps is mocking the thing under test; use RealLandingGitOps against a real git repo.\n\n### Add a Contract Tests section\n\nThe persona currently has a Contract Tests section at lines 102-106. Rewrite it to cover the \"stub must pass the real implementation's contract suite\" pattern, not just \"APIs meet specifications.\" Example structure:\n\n```go\n// contract_test.go — runs the same suite against both implementations\nfunc TestBeadStoreContract(t *testing.T) {\n    cases := []struct {\n        name  string\n        build func(t *testing.T) Store\n    }{\n        {\"memory\", func(t *testing.T) Store { return NewMemoryStore() }},\n        {\"file\",   func(t *testing.T) Store { return NewFileStore(t.TempDir()) }},\n    }\n    for _, c := range cases {\n        t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            s := c.build(t)\n            t.Run(\"CreateAndGet\", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })\n            t.Run(\"UpdateLosesNoFields\", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })\n            t.Run(\"ConcurrentWritersSerialized\", func(t *testing.T) { /* ... */ })\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\nIf the stub passes and the real backend fails, the backend is broken or the stub is lying. Either way the contract suite exposes the drift immediately. This is how DDx keeps stubs honest.\n\n### Add a Drift Signals subsection\n\nMirror the HELIX testing concern's drift signals and add DDx-specific anti-patterns. Any agent reviewing code that contains these should reject or refactor:\n\n- `testify/mock` imports in new test files\n- `gomock` generated mocks for first-party interfaces\n- Structs named `*Mock*`, `fake*Git`, `fake*Store`, `orchTest*GitOps`, `gateTest*GitOps` that implement a first-party interface for assertion purposes (in-memory stubs for first-party interfaces are OK and encouraged, as long as they are real implementations, not call recorders)\n- Per-goroutine mock git in concurrency tests (the shared-writer invariant is not being tested)\n- test.skip, .skip(), @Ignore, @Disabled without a linked issue\n- Mock-heavy tests that assert call order rather than observable state\n- Static JSON fixture files (use factory functions with fakers instead)\n- Tests named `test1`, `testHelper`, `it works` (rename to describe behavior)\n- `expect(true).toBe(true)` or tautological assertions\n- Commented-out tests (git has the history; delete them)\n- Retry loop around a flaky test (fix the flake)\n\n### Add citations to authoritative sources\n\nThe persona must explicitly cite:\n\n- **HELIX testing concern**: `workflows/concerns/testing/concern.md` in HELIX (or `.ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/concerns/testing/concern.md` when installed) — the upstream authority for the stubs-over-mocks rule.\n- **DDx concerns.md**: `docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md` — the DDx-specific application of the rule, including the zero-mocks-in-integration-tests override.\n- **Script harness**: `cli/internal/agent/script.go` — the canonical deterministic fake agent.\n- **Integration test reference**: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go` — the reference pattern that every new integration test should follow.\n\n### Update the installed copy\n\nlibrary/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the source of truth. The installed copy at .ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md must also be updated so downstream projects (HELIX and others) that depend on the DDx plugin pick up the fix. Either (a) update both files in the same commit and let the install process pick up the new source the next time it runs, or (b) run the library sync mechanism as part of the bead work.\n\n## Why this matters\n\nEvery test in DDx's history that was written by an agent following this persona produced mock-heavy code. Those tests passed CI, appeared to cover invariants, and silently proved nothing. The alpha14 → alpha16 session has been the first time anyone audited them end-to-end and discovered the drift. Without this fix, the next agent adopting the persona will reproduce the same broken pattern.\n\nThe cost of fixing the persona is hours. The cost of NOT fixing it is weeks of debugging \"why doesn't the land coordinator work\" when the real answer is \"because every test that 'proved' the old merge path worked was a lie.\"","id":"ddx-482fad2f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:library","area:docs","area:testing","kind:correctness"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-51e0142","status":"closed","title":"test-engineer-tdd persona contradicts testing concern (promotes mocks)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:43:14.958570875Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:374:{"acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Harness: claude / model: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:388:{"acceptance":"- [ ] --output flag accepts text | json-result | session-jsonl\n- [ ] Default is text\n- [ ] Integration test covers all three modes\n- [ ] No behavior change for session-jsonl mode","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:26:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"cbaee23432d7fe8167b5dd1974fc237dc20437a4","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.719835899Z","description":"ddx agent run currently prints the agent's full session (JSONL events) to stdout regardless of flags. To extract just the final assistant text message, callers have to parse JSONL and walk the session log. This breaks trivial automation like 'review=$(ddx agent run ... --prompt @/tmp/r.md)'.\n\n## Scope\n- --output text: emit only the final assistant text message to stdout (no tool calls, no JSONL)\n- --output json-result: emit only the result-type JSON object (session metadata + final text)\n- --output session-jsonl: current behavior; keep as non-default opt-in\n- Default switches to --output text\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: both review runs spilled 90-200KB of session JSONL to stdout, requiring python3 JSON-walking to extract the ~2KB opus verdict. --output text would have made the review invocation one line instead of three.","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on. See ddx-4b59b816 for the full scope.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:43.231414618Z","kind":"note","source":"cost-tiered work meta-epic cross-link 2026-04-15","summary":"Part of cost-tiered execution + measurement meta-epic ddx-4b59b816 — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar."},{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=cbaee23432d7fe8167b5dd1974fc237dc20437a4\nbase_rev=ba3c8e89d8c761cc7185304777b633b28945ffec","created_at":"2026-04-15T05:36:54.846376444Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:36:40.703736961Z","id":"ddx-518b2690","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:cli","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-dca40d97","status":"closed","title":"exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:36:54.864845558Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:394:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.\n\nProblem:\nThe plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.\n\nWhy this matters:\nThe docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.\n\nRequired fix:\nMake the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.\n\nAcceptance:\n- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.\n- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.\n- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.\n- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:844:{"acceptance":"- [ ] All six child beads are filed and actionable\n- [ ] The first three (fetch, push, rebase) ship as a coordinated release since none of them are useful without the others\n- [ ] A test harness exists that simulates two parallel workers on the same machine and asserts no merge commits are produced\n- [ ] A test harness exists that simulates two machines with divergent histories and asserts the second machine's claim is refused until reconciliation\n- [ ] docs/EXECUTE-LOOP.md (or equivalent) documents the fetch/rebase/push invariant and the multi-machine topology\n- [ ] The epic closes when all children close AND an end-to-end test has run the loop for 10 iterations across two simulated machines without divergence","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:18:37.269117551Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-83ec1430","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-6aa50e57","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2791dc4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-42dd982c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-b0374901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Root cause epic for the multi-machine divergence problem discovered on axon 2026-04-14 during a planning session. Execute-loop treats master as a private branch and produces divergent histories that never reconcile with origin.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, two machines running execute-loop (`eitri` and `sindri`) produced 14 days of independent history from a shared merge-base at 27c54ded (2026-04-06):\n\n- eitri: 134 local commits that never reached origin\n- sindri: 336 commits that pushed to origin\n- Same FEAT-026 slot used for two unrelated features (sync protocol on eitri, markdown templates on sindri)\n- Same beads.jsonl touched by both machines with divergent bead IDs and lifecycle state\n- `refs/ddx/iterations/` preservation never fired because merges kept succeeding textually while producing semantically incoherent state\n- 12+ \"Merge made by 'ort' strategy\" commits on 2026-04-13 afternoon on eitri alone, each bringing in a sibling local execute-bead worker's output\n\n## Root causes identified\n\n1. No `git fetch origin` at the top of a worker loop iteration — each bead starts from the worker's stale local HEAD (child bead DDX-agent-fetch)\n2. No `git push` on successful landing — successful work stays trapped on the local branch (child bead DDX-agent-push)\n3. Intra-machine sibling workers land via `git merge --no-edit` (ort strategy) instead of rebase, producing a fan of merge commits instead of a linear history (child bead DDX-agent-rebase)\n4. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger with no concurrent-write semantics (child bead DDX-agent-tracker)\n5. No divergence monitoring — the 134/336 split grew silently over two weeks (child bead DDX-agent-monitor)\n6. Multi-machine topology undocumented — which machine is authoritative, who pulls, who pushes (child bead DDX-agent-topology)\n\n## Primary source evidence\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 — Merge function uses `git merge --no-edit` with 'ort' strategy, no rebase alternative, commits a 'chore: checkpoint before merge' before attempting each merge\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go — has ZERO references to push/fetch/origin\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:~320 — worker loop runs Executor.Execute without any fetch-origin or ancestry-check step\n\n## Impact\n\nAny project that has run the execute-loop for more than a few days across more than one machine is silently divergent from origin. Content written by this session cannot be pushed to the axon origin without a multi-hour manual reconciliation. The axon repo currently has two weeks of unshared work on two machines.\n\n## Scope of this epic\n\nSix child beads covering: fetch-before-work, push-on-success, rebase-vs-merge, tracker concurrency, divergence monitoring, multi-machine topology docs. Each is actionable and self-contained.\n\n## Blocks\n\nAny further `ddx agent execute-loop` runs on projects with more than one participating machine should be paused until at least the first three child beads (fetch, push, rebase) are landed. Running the loop in the current state compounds divergence.","id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","issue_type":"epic","labels":["bug","execute-loop","orchestration","epic"],"notes":"## Reframed 2026-04-14 — design anchor: ddx-8746d8a6\n\nThe six original child beads (bd42f640, 6aa50e57, 83ec1430, 2791dc4f, 42dd982c, b0374901) identified pieces of the gap correctly but did not compose into a coherent end-to-end design. Per an opus-led architectural review on 2026-04-14, the root problem is not that execute-loop's git operations are missing pieces; it is that the whole model is wrong.\n\nTarget model (from the ddx user): human-PR queue. Workers are contributors, a centralized land coordinator is the maintainer, target-ref writes are serialized through a single writer that always sees the current tip.\n\nThe design for this model is in **ddx-8746d8a6 \"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end (meta redesign)\"**. Treat ddx-8746d8a6 as the authoritative design anchor for this epic. The per-bug children below continue to describe individual gap points, but their FIX is the coordinator pattern, not piecemeal patches:\n\n- ddx-bd42f640 (fetch+ancestry before claim) → subsumed by the coordinator's fetch step\n- ddx-6aa50e57 (rebase instead of merge) → implemented by the coordinator's rebase step (add land-lock note to acceptance)\n- ddx-83ec1430 (push on landing) → part of the coordinator's sequence\n- ddx-42dd982c (divergence monitoring) → reframed as coordinator rejection/conflict metrics\n- ddx-b0374901 (multi-machine docs) → reframed as per-machine coordinator + push-to-origin\n- ddx-2791dc4f (tracker concurrency) → STAYS SEPARATE — tracker is a second shared resource with its own lock story\n\nNew P0 child filed alongside: **ddx-e14efc58 \"server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost\"** — acute correctness bug discovered during the review. The server path today drops worker commits entirely. Interim fix mirrors the --local closure's land sequence; long-term fix is absorbed by ddx-8746d8a6.\n\nRollout: implement ddx-8746d8a6 first (the coordinator), then close the subsumed children as their work is done, then close this epic. Do NOT try to land the individual children as standalone patches — the piecemeal approach is what got us here.\n\nDo NOT run execute-loop against the ddx repo (or any other repo) under this epic's pattern until the coordinator is in place. Record from the user: \"we may not be separating concerns appropriately — worker should be 'I've been handed a worktree and a task, I will execute that task and make commits, produce progress output, notify done.'\"","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"Epic: execute-loop git orchestration bugs cause multi-machine divergence","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:16.842137559Z"}

Normal ddx run/try/work execution paths never call ResolveRoute and never re-inject RouteDecision values into Execute.\n5. 28c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c4231775","depends_on_id":"ddx-d4100b7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c4231775","depends_on_id":"ddx-da19756a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"}],"description":"## Goal\n\nSimplify DDx agent execution so DDx does not participate in model/provider/harness routing while still owning bead retry policy.\n\nDDx may:\n- estimate work effort;\n- request abstract agent power bounds using MinPower and optional MaxPower;\n- accept operator passthrough constraints (`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`) and send them unchanged to the agent;\n- inspect the agent's available model/power catalog only to choose abstract MinPower thresholds, including top-model-only retries;\n- decide whether a bead attempt succeeded using DDx-owned evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, post-run gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and run metadata;\n- retry a bead with higher MinPower when policy permits;\n- pass non-routing execution intent to the agent: prompt/workdir, power bounds, effort/reasoning, permissions, timeouts, session/evidence metadata, passthrough constraints, and task facts such as EstimatedPromptTokens or RequiresTools.\n\nThe agent owns all routing inside the power bounds and passthrough constraints: harness/provider/model validation, endpoint choice, candidate scoring, health, quota, fallback, compatibility errors, and route errors. The agent reports actual model and actual power used, e.g. `running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- No legacy compatibility paths for DDx-side ladders, model overrides, profiles, or default-path preflight routing.\n- No DDx interpretation of harness/provider/model passthrough values.\n- No DDx-owned provider probing/filtering in execution setup.\n- No candidate traces in execution evidence from ResolveRoute.\n- No cheap/standard/smart profile taxonomy in DDx execution policy.\n\n## Required command layering\n\n1. `ddx run`: one invocation atom. Calls agent Execute exactly once with requested MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints. Does not call ResolveRoute.\n2. `ddx try \u003cbead\u003e`: one bead attempt. Builds bead prompt/worktree/evidence context, estimates initial or retry MinPower, carries passthrough constraints unchanged, then calls run.\n3. `ddx work`: queue drain and retry policy. Picks beads, invokes try, classifies results, and schedules higher-MinPower retries when policy permits. It does not inspect passthrough constraint values.\n\n## Passthrough envelope rule\n\nHarness/provider/model fields must live in one narrow agent-passthrough envelope. DDx may copy the envelope between layers and persist it for audit. DDx must not validate, branch on, fallback, rewrite, widen, or use these fields for queue/retry policy. If retry MinPower changes, the passthrough envelope remains unchanged and the agent resolves compatibility.\n\nIf hard pins make requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx stops with a typed terminal classification such as `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent evidence, and reports operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, choose alternatives, call ResolveRoute, or keep escalating.\n\n## Retry eligibility\n\nDDx may raise MinPower only for classified outcomes where stronger reasoning could plausibly help after a valid attempt started, such as capability-insufficient results, post-run check failures after an attempted change, review-blocked capability failures, or no-changes-after-attempt within the no-progress budget.\n\nDDx must not use power retry for deterministic setup/operator-action failures: dirty worktree, merge or land conflict, missing checkout, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing harness binaries, auth failures, command-not-found/toolchain setup, passthrough exhaustion, or any case that would require inspecting/removing/replacing passthrough pins.\n\n## Guardrails\n\nAllowed raw passthrough touchpoints are CLI parsing, the single AgentPassthrough/AgentExecutionIntent value, Execute request construction, execution-record persistence, and human-facing evidence rendering. Retry/work/queue/catalog-threshold code carries the opaque value and may not read Harness, Provider, or Model fields. A routinglint/package-boundary check must enforce this allowlist.\n\nCatalog/status rendering is observability only. Retry threshold policy may consume abstract catalog power numbers to compute MinPower thresholds. Status/catalog code must not feed concrete model/provider/harness choices into execution.\n\n## Documentation corpus gate\n\nThis cleanup is not complete when only FEAT-006/FEAT-010 and the DDx skill are updated. The active documentation corpus must be scrubbed so operators, agents, and future implementation beads do not learn stale routing surfaces from older feature docs, test plans, technical designs, solution designs, or operator guides.\n\nActive governing docs must describe `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Remaining references to `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, quorum/profile flags, or DDx-side routing vocabulary must be either removed from active docs or explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context with a reason. A grep-based docs check must make this enforceable.\n\n## Review findings incorporated\n\n- ddx-da19756a: execution path reinjects informational RouteDecision.\n- ddx-d4100b7e: execution request drops DDx-owned power intent and actual-power evidence.\n- ddx-1997b22d: ddx work preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface.\n- ddx-b6a2417b: FEAT-006/specs still bless DDx-side ladders and overrides.\n- ddx-a65fea8a: DDx service adapter performs endpoint/model policy.\n- ddx-0f42c934: route-status scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence.\n- ddx-d06ae28c: live symptom where default work routes to cheap/incorrect model.\n- ddx-6036e1bc: status/catalog work must remain observability and power-threshold only.\n- ddx-8e9a11c4: plan aligned to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract.\n- ddx-52ce6249: public docs/skills use run/try/work instead of legacy agent run/execute surfaces.\n- ddx-6232daf5: retry eligibility matrix added.\n- ddx-b74d4ac6: passthrough exhaustion stop semantics added.\n- ddx-b0d96cb9: mechanical passthrough ownership guardrails required.\n- ddx-8a617e91: catalog/status display separated from retry threshold policy.\n- ddx-02335e0b: repo-wide active docs corpus scrub is a first-class gate.\n\n## Execution order\n\n1. Align specs/docs and test expectations to the MinPower/MaxPower boundary with passthrough constraints.\n2. Scrub the active docs corpus and add the docs grep allowlist/check so stale legacy command/routing guidance cannot remain hidden outside the core feature docs.\n3. Pull the upstream agent power-bounds interface release: Execute accepts MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough constraints, responses report actual model/power, and model catalog exposes available powers.\n4. Remove ResolveRoute and route preflight from run/try/work execution paths.\n5. Pass MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough envelope through ServiceExecuteRequest and persist requested bounds, actual power/model, and requested passthrough in run evidence.\n6. Replace work alias/execute-loop surface with run \u003c- try \u003c- work layering.\n7. Implement DDx retry power policy using the eligibility matrix and agent catalog power thresholds, without inspecting passthrough values.\n8. Remove DDx endpoint/model policy from execution adapter.\n9. Rescope route-status/provider UI candidate traces to status/debug only.\n10. Add routinglint and regression tests that forbid ResolveRoute/model-policy code in execution paths while allowing passthrough envelope plumbing.","id":"ddx-c4231775","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-006","feat-010","area:agent","area:routing","power-only"],"notes":"Amendment incorporated: --harness/--provider/--model are allowed as passthrough-only agent constraints. The structured REVIEW_STATUS verdict is never captured — we pay for the review cycle but lose its decision (block / approve / approve-with-followups).\n2. The salvage path masks the fact that reviews are not actually gating anything.\n3. Operators reading summary lines see rc=1 'failures' that are actually invisible reviews — confusing signal.\n\nSample log lines from /Users/erik/Projects/heimq/.helix-logs/helix-20260426-144028.log (run launched 14:40 UTC, completed 16:28 UTC):\n\n  --- ddx agent run [14:51:08] harness=claude rc=0 exit=1 elapsed=350s cost=/bin/zsh tokens=0 ---\n  --- end ddx agent output ---\n  helix: review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer\n  helix: review output was not safely interpretable after cycle 1 — continuing\n\nThis pattern repeated for every single one of the 12 cycles (same 0-tokens, same 'review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer' message after each cycle's review step).\n\nHypotheses to investigate:\n- Review prompt template emitted to Claude does not actually instruct the agent to print a REVIEW_STATUS trailer in a parseable form.\n- Prompt does instruct, but the trailer format the harness expects has drifted from what the prompt asks for.\n- Claude's output is being truncated before the trailer (timeouts? token caps?).\n- Review agent invocation is silently failing very early (0 tokens reported suggests it never got far) — could be a misconfigured CLI invocation, missing flag, env var.\n\nInvestigation steps:\n1. Inspect what prompt/system message the helix harness sends to claude for the review step — confirm it asks for a REVIEW_STATUS line.\n2. Inspect what the harness regexes for to detect the trailer.\n3. Run one review step manually with verbose logging to capture full agent stdout.\n4. Compare with codex agent — does the trailer issue exist there too, or is it claude-specific?\n\nWhy this matters: helix-run's correctness model assumes reviews actually gate progression. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1019:{"acceptance":"- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure\n- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier \u003e= impl tier, defaulting to smart\n- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown\n- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead\n- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible\n- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented\n- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation\n- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c84e9e6fc5e69cf00878adc228f621dedc22ea83","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.621451901Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-a317208c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-44084b3e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36Z"}],"description":"The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.\n\n## Principle\n\nDiscovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.\n\n## Default policy (no flags)\n\n- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead\n- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close\n- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry\n- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)\n- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.\n\n## Resource discovery at attempt time\n\n- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)\n- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier\n- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate\n- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)\n\n## Rare overrides\n\n- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)\n- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)\n- --harness \u003cname\u003e / --model \u003cname\u003e (debug override; disables escalation)\n\n## Surface\n\nDefault invocation becomes:\n\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once\n\nEverything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.\n\n## Events\n\nEach attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop:\n- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.\n- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.\n- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.\n\n## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on. Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:13-16 delegates to ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir).Log(runID) reading real run bundle. When available, splits by newlines, emits each as ExecutionEvent with seq-based EventID, then closes channel. ONE-SHOT dump (waits for run completion, then streams all lines at once) — not live tail. Acceptable given schema name is \"executionEvidence\" (final artifact), not \"executionTail\". Limitation worth documenting upstream.\n\nCOORDINATORMETRICS: resolver_sub_exec.go:96-150 polls CoordMetrics.GetCoordinatorMetrics(projectRoot) every MetricsPollInterval (default 1s). Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:24-37 calls reg.MetricsFor(projectRoot) on real workers.LandCoordinators registry. Diff with prev via snap != last, emits only on change. 1s interval appropriate for land events (seconds-to-minutes timescale).\n\nBOTH TESTS REAL: httptest.NewServer + full graphql-transport-ws handshake. Coordinator test uses atomic.Pointer[CoordinatorMetricsSnap] flipped mid-test to simulate registry updates — verifies diff emission across snap1 -\u003e snap2.\n\nGRAPHQL_ADAPTERS.GO: proper import-cycle workaround. graphql package defines ExecLogProvider/CoordinatorMetricsProvider interfaces + CoordinatorMetricsSnap DTO; outer server package provides concrete implementations (execLogAdapter, coordMetricsAdapter). Standard pattern for nested resolver wiring.\n\nMINOR (non-blocking):\n1. executionEvidence polls forever until ctx cancels if run never completes — correct but worth docs\n2. 3 separate test helpers (newGraphQLHandlerWithBeadBus/ExecLogs/CoordMetrics) duplicate ~90 lines of setup — cosmetic dedupe opportunity (note in refactor bead)\n\nVERDICT: ACCEPT.","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:05:22.694240511Z","kind":"review","source":"opus batch review (Stage 2.11 + 2.12)","summary":"ACCEPT: both subscriptions poll real data sources with diff-based emission. executionEvidence streams real ddxexec.Store log once after run completes (500ms poll for availability); coordinatorMetrics polls real workers.LandCoordinators registry every 1s with snapshot diff. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1053:{"acceptance":"(1) ClosureGate in cli/internal/bead enforces close invariants ONLY on the automated execution path (execute-loop + Store.CloseWithEvidence): terminal verdict event present (kind=review with APPROVE + non-empty rationale, or kind=review-skipped with reason), execution-evidence event present (base_rev + result_rev). Gate applies to CloseWithEvidence; it does not wrap Store.Close (manual tracker administration remains a separate concern).\n(2) Empty-bodied APPROVE verdicts rejected by the gate with a clear error. This is the axon-c5cc071a failure mode.\n(3) Table-driven unit test covers at minimum: no execution event, no verdict, malformed verdict, success without result_rev, APPROVE with empty rationale.\n(4) Replay of axon-c5cc071a's closure scenario through CloseWithEvidence rejects the close.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:03:55.928477017Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-e30e60a9","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:12Z"}],"description":"Sibling to ddx-738edf47. Codex review of the sequencing plan flagged that the false-closure bug has a broader fix than just its pinpoint: the execute-loop has no invariant check enforcing that every `status: closed` transition carries execution evidence plus a parseable terminal verdict (review APPROVE, review SKIPPED_BY_FLAG, or manual close).\n\nReporter context: axon bead `axon-c5cc071a` closed in 4m33s with `events: []`, no bundle, no commit — the closure path was walked without any gate.\n\n## Work\n\n1. Identify every call site in cli/internal/agent and cli/internal/server that transitions a bead to `status: closed`. At minimum: execute-loop happy path, reviewer APPROVE path, `--no-review` skip path, manual `ddx bead close`.\n2. Centralize closure through a single `ClosureGate` (or similar) that refuses to close unless:\n   - execute-bead has a success event referencing a base_rev/result_rev pair, AND\n   - a terminal verdict event exists (kind=review with summary APPROVE, or kind=review-skipped with an explicit reason, or kind=manual-close with actor set).\n3. Add a table-driven invariant test that constructs synthetic bead states (missing execution evidence, missing verdict, malformed verdict, manual close) and asserts the gate's verdict.\n4. Explicit escape hatch for `ddx bead close --force` (manual operator close) — logged with actor + reason, bypasses the gate.\n\n## Relationship to ddx-738edf47\n\n738edf47 is the specific false-closure symptom report + suspected root cause. This bead is the structural invariant test that catches similar regressions regardless of which upstream component (reviewer, execute-loop, worker) walks the close path incorrectly. Both can land together; this one owns the test harness, 738edf47 owns the repro fix.","id":"ddx-e30e60a9","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:test","kind:correctness","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: invariant test — no bead close without execution evidence + valid review/skip verdict","updated_at":"2026-04-20T16:23:03.626644116Z"}

The document contains a 'Current Gaps' table with at least the 4 confirmed gaps from epic ddx-2d974641 and links to the tracking beads.\n\n4. FEAT-006 and skills/ddx/reference/agents.md each have a one-line pointer to the new doc.\n\n5. **CI grep verifies citations resolve** (per opus fresh-eyes review). Failing citations block the PR.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:37:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"790f3b9752c1b3b289fd8f3725291fd5b16ed067","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:06:41.260303435Z","description":"## Problem\n\nNo single document traces the provider/model/harness resolution path from CLI flags to the final HTTP request. SD-015 and SD-023 are SPECS — they describe what should happen in 5 routing modes. The code lives across routing.go, discovery.go, agent_runner.go, runner.go, and agent_cmd.go. A newcomer debugging a routing bug must infer the flow by reading all of them.\n\nThe audit for epic ddx-2d974641 turned up a RouteRequest struct missing its Provider field, and a parallel RunOptions.Provider path that bypasses routing — neither of which is documented. This is the kind of hidden structural defect a Resolution Path document would have flagged on first read.\n\n## Fix\n\nWrite docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (or similar location; coordinate with SD-015's author). The document traces one concrete invocation end-to-end:\n\n    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n\nWalk through every layer, with file:line citations:\n\n1. **CLI parse** — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go flag extraction (line references).\n\n2. **RouteFlags -\u003e RouteRequest normalization** — routing.go:NormalizeRouteRequest. What each CLI flag maps to; which flags are discarded when they don't match an expected shape; the current gap around --provider.\n\n3. **Discovery probe** — discovery.go:DiscoverProviderModels. When it fires; what it caches; the 30s TTL; how failures are handled.\n\n4. **Candidate planning** — routing.go:BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate. For each registered harness, the state map lookup and the viability checks.\n\n5. **Fuzzy matching** — discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel. Pool selection (global vs per-provider), normalization (current gaps), prefix + tiebreak algorithm.\n\n6. **Scoring** — routing.go:scoreCandidate. Per-profile scoring, cost class, historical success rate, provider-affinity bonus (when implemented).\n\n7. **Candidate ranking** — routing.go:RankCandidates / SelectBestCandidate. Non-viable rejection, stable tiebreak.\n\n8. **Dispatch** — runner.go:Run. Harness-specific branches (virtual, agent, script, HTTP-provider-embedded, default exec). For embedded: agent_runner.go:RunAgent -\u003e resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider -\u003e agent library call.\n\n9. **Wire-level request** — ddx-agent builds the HTTP body (system_prompt + messages + tools), sends to the provider. What the server receives.\n\n10. **Response parsing** — streaming or non-streaming decode; where 'unexpected end of JSON input' (ddx-6a5dfe35) surfaces.\n\nInclude a SEQUENCE DIAGRAM (mermaid or ASCII) showing the layers.\n\nInclude a GAP TABLE at the top listing every currently-missing piece (as of whatever the SD-015 implementation state is) with links to the specific beads tracking each parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n  `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n  value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n  the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n  viability gate. Checks:\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1082:{"acceptance":"RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead takes a fromRev parameter. After read-tree HEAD + update-ref, the main worktree reflects the new HEAD for exactly the files changed by the landing commit (no phantom D or M entries), while unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved. Both Land() fast-path and merge-path call sites pass the correct fromRev. FetchOriginAncestryCheck same. Interface + test stubs updated. go build ./... passes. go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 passes. Manual verification: after the next worker land, running `git status --short` in the main worktree shows zero phantom entries related to the landing commit.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T22:07:13Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3722863","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:07:07.402571523Z","description":"RealLandingGitOps.SyncIndexToHead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go only runs `git read-tree HEAD` after update-ref, which syncs the INDEX to HEAD but does NOT update files on disk in the main worktree. Every worker-landed commit that adds or modifies a tracked file leaves the main worktree showing phantom \"D\" or \"M\" entries in git status, even though HEAD is correct.\n\nImpact during the Svelte+GraphQL migration epic: opus reviewers have been misled multiple times (reading stale working-tree copies instead of HEAD blobs), and I have manually run `git checkout -- \u003cpath\u003e` after nearly every worker close (~6+ times this session). Subsequent agents that read files from the worktree see old content.\n\nScope of fix:\n1. SyncIndexToHead -\u003e SyncWorkTreeToHead. Take a fromRev parameter.\n2. After read-tree HEAD, compute `git diff --name-only \u003cfromRev\u003e HEAD` to find the exact files changed by the landing commit\n3. Run `git checkout-index -f -- \u003cfiles\u003e` to restore only those files (preserves unrelated local modifications like agent-logs and beads.jsonl heartbeats)\n4. Apply to both call sites in Land() (fast-forward path and merge path) and the FetchOriginAncestryCheck update-ref block\n5. Update LandingGitOps interface + test stubs\n\nAlternative considered: git checkout-index -a -f (restore ALL files from index) — rejected because it would clobber legitimate local modifications to files unrelated to the landing commit (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat writes).\n\nThis is a P0 blocker for running the migration epic without constant human babysitting. Fix directly via deterministic edit, not via worker dispatch, because the surface is small, the fix is mechanical, and the diagnostic cost of a worker misunderstanding the fix is higher than the implementation cost of just applying it.","events":[{"actor":"deterministic-fix","body":"Commit: 94f0a91\n\nRenamed LandingGitOps.SyncIndexToHead -\u003e SyncWorkTreeToHead with a new fromRev parameter. Implementation:\n1. read-tree HEAD (syncs index — preserved)\n2. git diff --name-only fromRev HEAD (enumerates the minimum set of files changed by the update-ref)\n3. Partition: index-present files go through git checkout-index -f --; deleted-in-HEAD files go through os.Remove\n\nThis preserves unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat, operator scratch) by passing the exact file list rather than using checkout-index -a -f.\n\nCall sites updated:\n- Land() fast-forward path -\u003e fromRev=currentTip\n- Land() merge path -\u003e fromRev=currentTip\n- FetchOriginAncestryCheck origin-ahead branch -\u003e fromRev=localSHA\n\nTest coverage:\n- TestLand_HappyPath_FastForward now asserts that feature.txt is materialized on disk AND git status --porcelain is clean for that path after Land() returns. This is the regression assertion for the bug.\n- Existing Land unit tests + integration tests + coordinator tests all pass.\n\nBuild + vet + test clean.","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:12:42.021548811Z","kind":"completion","source":"manual fix during migration epic","summary":"Fixed in commit 94f0a91: SyncIndexToHead renamed to SyncWorkTreeToHead with fromRev parameter, materializes only files changed by the landing commit, preserves unrelated local modifications"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T22:07:13.6833208Z","id":"ddx-eaebaffb","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:git","priority:p0","blocker:epic-migration"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"bug: Land() leaves main worktree stale — update-ref + read-tree doesn't materialize files","updated_at":"2026-04-14T22:12:42.101976649Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1100:{"acceptance":"AC1. Land-coordinator refuses to merge a bead result whose net diff deletes more than N lines from a single file (configurable threshold, default 200) UNLESS the agent's no_changes_rationale.txt or commit message explicitly acknowledges and justifies the deletion (e.g. 'intentional file removal: \u003creason\u003e').\n\nAC2. Land-coordinator runs a basic sanity gate before merging: the file must still parse for its filetype (TypeScript files compile, Svelte files have valid \u003cscript\u003e/\u003ctemplate\u003e/\u003cstyle\u003e structure if they had one previously, JSON is valid JSON, etc.). Failed parse = preserve as iteration ref, do not merge.\n\nAC3. When a parse-fail or large-deletion gate trips, the bead is marked 'preserved-needs-review' and the operator is alerted via the bead's notes field, with a link to the iteration ref and a one-line summary of what gate fired.\n\nAC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.\n\nAC6. A regression test on the harness validates that an attempt to merge a result deleting \u003eN lines (or producing a parse failure) is correctly preserved+rejected, not merged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3881188","closing_commit_sha":"77715556bbfaf9ed63d5b9aa0d23da6ca724b02f","created_at":"2026-04-30T01:17:03.251053789Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-fd31b628","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e2a51cd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e19c5181","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-b6c42808","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:54Z"}],"description":"Today on axon repo, the ddx-land-coordinator merged a bead-result branch onto master that deleted 1448 lines from one Svelte component (1552 → 104 lines). The same merge also corrupted a second test file (1043 → 158 lines, overwritten with unrelated content). Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its \u003cscript lang=\"ts\"\u003e wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.\n\nThe damaging merge:\n\n  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075\n  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2\n  Author: ddx-land-coordinator \u003ccoordinator@ddx.local\u003e\n  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400\n\n      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master\n\n       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------\n       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)\n\nThe post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.\n\nNet damage on master before manual recovery:\n  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)\n  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)\n  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start\n\nManual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.\n\nWhy this happened (hypothesis):\n  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).\n  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).\n  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.\n\nOr alternatively: the agent's tool calls deleted content from the worktree without re-emitting the full file (e.g. used Edit with overlapping replace blocks that effectively truncated), the harness committed whatever was on disk without sanity-checking magnitude, and the land-coordinator merged the partial content as authoritative.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44.162888426Z","id":"ddx-efe058be","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:land-coordinator","kind:safety","severity:critical"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: land-coordinator merged a result that deleted 1448 lines from a single file (catastrophic damage)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:22:05.677486788Z"}

Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.\n\n1. StateProvider interface split\n   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.\n\n2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)\n   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64(\"cursor:\u003cslice-index\u003e\"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.\n\n3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types\n   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.\n\n4. r.State == nil nil-check\n   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.\n\nAvailable paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:\n1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)\n2. `ddx agent workers stop \u003cworker-id\u003e` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)\n3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work\n4. Manual edit of server-state.json risks corruption and isn't documented\n\nThe bead itself wasn't blocked (status=open, owner=null) so the registry noise is mostly cosmetic — it pollutes `ddx agent workers` output and confuses operators trying to triage the queue. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1131:{"acceptance":"(1) Reviewer verdict extraction consumes NORMALIZED reviewer text (the harness's final cleaned response), not raw stream frames. If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).\n(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.\n(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.\n(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:55:04.187171368Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f7ae036f","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness\n\nThis bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a \"review-error reopens per closed bead\" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.\n\n---\n\nReporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.\n\nThe post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}` in `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.\n\nCode site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\\s+Verdict:\\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{...\"text\":\"### Verdict: APPROVE...\"}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 (\"safe default: requires human attention\"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail \"post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)\".\n\nActual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.\n\nHypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.\n\n## Upstream dependency\n\nBlocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on \"normalized reviewer text\" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.\n\n## Scope split (wave 2)\n\nClauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.\n\nClause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.","id":"ddx-f7ae036f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","kind:review","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer","scope:partial"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:50:37.451719605Z"}
